$comment: RMA/FSA deadline rules — Illinois, 2026 crop year, corn & soybeans. Dates compiled from RMA county actuarial documents and FSA program calendars; VERIFY against the RMA Actuarial Information Browser and county FSA office before relying on them for a real filing. Rules are data, not code (ARCHITECTURE.md §5): refresh annually.

rules.title: Sales closing / policy change deadline
rules.description: Last day to buy a new crop insurance policy or change coverage level, plan, or options for the 2026 crop year.
rules.consequence: Miss it and you're locked into last year's coverage decisions for the entire year.
rules.source: RMA county actuarial documents (IL, spring-planted row crops)

rules.title: ARC/PLC election & enrollment
rules.description: Elect and enroll in ARC-County, ARC-Individual, or PLC for 2026 covered commodities at your county FSA office.
rules.consequence: No enrollment means no ARC/PLC payment for the year even if prices or county revenue collapse.
rules.source: FSA ARC/PLC program calendar — confirm exact 2026 date with county office

rules.title: Final planting date — corn
rules.description: Last day to plant corn with full insurance guarantee. A 25-day late-planting period follows with the guarantee reduced ~1%/day; prevented-planting claims key off this date.
rules.consequence: Planting after this date without documentation reduces or jeopardizes your guarantee.
rules.source: RMA IL actuarial documents (most IL counties; a few southern counties differ)

rules.title: Final planting date — soybeans
rules.description: Last day to plant soybeans with full insurance guarantee; 25-day late-planting period follows.

rules.title: Acreage reporting deadline
rules.description: Report planted acres by crop, field, and practice to both your insurance agent (RMA) and county FSA office. Neumeric assembles this from your field and crop-season records.
rules.consequence: Unreported acres are uninsured acres, and FSA program eligibility depends on the report.
rules.source: RMA/FSA common acreage reporting date for IL spring-planted crops

rules.title: Premium billing date
rules.description: Crop insurance premium billing issued; interest accrues on unpaid premium after ~30 days.
rules.consequence: Late payment accrues interest and can affect coverage standing.

rules.title: End of insurance period — corn
rules.description: Latest date insurance attaches to unharvested corn; harvest or file notice before this date.
rules.consequence: Losses discovered after the end of the insurance period are not covered.

rules.title: Production reporting deadline (2026 crop)
rules.description: Report 2026 actual production to your agent — this sets your APH yield history, which sets next year's guarantee.
rules.consequence: Missing or sloppy production reports permanently drag down the yield history your future coverage is built on.
rules.source: RMA production reporting date for IL spring crops

rules.title: Notice of loss — 72-hour rule
rules.description: Give notice to your agent within 72 hours of discovering damage (hail, flood, wind, disease), and before destroying or harvesting the damaged crop. Photograph and document first — this is the moment Neumeric's evidence capture exists for.
rules.consequence: Late notice is one of the most common reasons claims get reduced or denied.
rules.source: Common Crop Insurance Policy basic provisions
